name: Soda Cracker Candy
recipeInstructions:
For all you Heath bar fans out here, you ought to love this one. It's really easy and doesn't heat up your kitchen...and it's a hit with young and old alike.
Melt butter and sugar on the stove over medium heat until bubbly, then cook and stir for 5 minutes. Line a 9 X 13 pan with soda crackers, placing them in a single layer. Pour the sugar/butter mixture over the crackers. Use a knife to keep the crackers in place as they tend to float. Sprinkle a small bag (6 oz) of semisweet chocolate chips over the top of the mixture and spread evenly with a spatula. Refrigerate for about 40 minutes until firm, then cut into squares. If they are firm enough, remove them from the pan; otherwise, return them to the fridge to set further so they become hard.
